article_items.title: How Justin Ernest invested nearly $500M into hot startups without a traditional VC fund | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Instead of spending a year raising a formal venture fund, the Sabertooth VC founder used a captive network of LPs to invest in startups like Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX.
article_items.excerpt: Instead of spending a year raising a formal venture fund, the Sabertooth VC founder used a captive network of LPs to invest in startups like Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX.
article_items.summary: Sabertooth has already had one major big return from chipmaker Groq, which was licensed and acqui-hired by Nvidia for $20 billion late last year. Having spent over five years at Playground Global investing in deep tech and helping lead fundraising, Ernest was confident his connections to both investors and founders would allow him to bridge that gap. “He has judgment, he has expertise, he’s very technical, that really distinguishes him from other organizations that tend to, in my opinion, just trying to aggregate capital.” When Wagner tried to invest directly in PsiQuantum, the quantum computing startup last valued at $7 billion, the company’s CFO suggested that he invest through Sabertooth. He’s writing checks ranging from $10 million to $275 million - meaning he’s. Next up is SpaceX’s highly anticipated IPO this Friday, along with Anthropic’s expected public listing later this year. Having spent over five years at Playground Global investing in deep tech and helping lead fundraising, Ernest was confident his connections to both investors and founders would allow him to bridge that gap.
Instead of spending a year raising a formal venture fund, the Sabertooth VC founder used a captive network of LPs to invest in startups like Anthropic, Anduril, and SpaceX.

WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence, and more Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today Waymo bought Apple’s self-driving car proving ground for.
Over the last 12 months, his firm, Sabertooth Capital , has invested nearly $500 million into 10 companies, including Anthropic, Anduril, Base Power, Databricks, PsiQuantum, and SpaceX.
“I wanted to be in the action,” he said.
However, Ernest quickly raised a significant amount of cash from them because, in the sometimes-shady world of small allocations and SPVs targeting family offices, he’s earned a solid reputation.
Ernest credits his ability to secure allocations of stock when highly coveted tech companies are raising to his wide network.

“I can usually make four or five or six phone calls, and I know exactly what my LPs will commit.” Ernest told TechCrunch that for now, he wants to continue growing his business of raising funds for specific companies on.
At a time when startups like Anthropic and Anduril are cracking down on unauthorized SPVs , investing through Sabertooth gives smaller limited partners some peace of mind.
“Justin is authentically an investor,” said Benjamin Wagner, a CIO for a family office managing the wealth of 50 individuals.
Marina Temkin is a venture capital and startups reporter at TechCrunch.

Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is going to be a big hit with the web's vibe coders.
The snake never stops moving, and if you run off the screen, you die.
Ethan Mollick, a notable AI researcher and University of Pennsylvania scholar, has been playing around with the model and seems to be having a lot of fun.
The implications are pretty clear.
As for founders and operators watching AI capability curves, it’s a useful data point about how quickly the floor is rising.

I like the animation here best - the player is a lone figure in a nocturnal landscape - although there isn’t much to the gameplay other than walking around while Rilke passages materialize on the screen.
The graphics look like a degraded version of Myst - they aren’t great - but the fact that the game exists at all, generated from a single prompt, is impressive.

He added that it was “capable across many problems and produced some startling results - it would work up to a dozen hours executing on multi-page specifications.” Perhaps most strikingly, Mollick used Fable to create a.
It’s reason for vibe coders of the world to rejoice.

article_items.title: Siri AI first look: How Apple's rebuilt AI-powered assistant behaves across iPhone, iPad and Mac - Engadget
article_items.description: Here's your first look at Siri AI, and how it works across Apple's devices.
article_items.excerpt: Here's your first look at Siri AI, and how it works across Apple's devices.
article_items.summary: Those who don't want to mess around with potentially buggy software can wait until the fall, when Apple typically releases the latest versions of its various operating systems to the public. After you install the iOS 27 beta , you can register your desire to check out Siri AI in the Apple Intelligence portion of the Settings app. It's something that will most likely change by the time the public beta arrives, or when Siri AI rolls out generally. Finally, another change in the way you access Siri is in context menus. Siri AI first look: How Apple's rebuilt AI-powered assistant behaves across iPhone, iPad and Mac The biggest news out of WWDC 2026 was, as widely rumored, the next generation of Siri.

Those who don't want to mess around with potentially buggy software can wait until the fall, when Apple typically releases the latest versions of its various operating systems to the public.

You bring it up the same way you would switch from Photo to Video to Portrait modes - that is, by swiping through the options at the bottom of the viewfinder until you land on "Siri." On Macs, there are two new keyboard.
By the way, I saw this gesture on both iPhones and iPads, and on the latter the animation is quite delightful.
For now, you'll see a new Ask Siri option at the top of this menu in macOS, while the Ask Siri option is at the bottom of the context menu in iPadOS.
You were always able to access Visual Intelligence by long-pressing the Camera Control button or the Action button if you had programmed it that way.
One of the main differences is the new onscreen gesture you can use to invoke Siri.
Then, the Spotlight search bar appeared with the words "Search to ask" in it.
Instead of double tapping the bottom of the display to type to Siri, you now swipe down from the top center, just as you would have to pull down Spotlight search in the past.
In the demos I saw, and in the rare instances where I tried to ask my own questions, the system was fairly responsive.

On the demo devices that I saw, there were lots of cards with titles like "San Francisco Parks for Kids," "NYC Buildings over 1,000 feet" or "Dog Domestication Timeline." Above each title was a time or day, and the.

Amazon Taps Fiber Optics Producer Corning for Data Center Expansion Amazon Taps Fiber Optics Producer Corning for Data Center Expansion The agreement follows AI infrastructure deals between Corning and Nvidia and.
Corning will supply the optical fiber, cables, and connectivity systems needed to enable rapid data sharing across racks and chips in data centers.
In May, Nvidia invested $3.2B in Corning , as part of a deal that will see the fiber-optics company build three new advanced manufacturing plants in North Carolina and Texas for the AI chipmaker.
The deal, made public on June 8, will span several years and is expected to generate 1,000 jobs at Corning’s North Carolina facilities.
The deal is framed as a boost to U.S.
The development also adds to growing industry interest in the glass and fiber optics industry.
Amazon has signed a multibillion-dollar agreement with fiber-optics manufacturer Corning in an infrastructure deal intended to support the e-commerce giant’s data center expansion in the U.S.
“Corning’s fiber optics are a critical piece of that infrastructure, and together, these investments help fuel the U.S.
Scarlett Evans is a freelance writer with a focus on emerging technologies and the minerals industry.
Scarlett also has a background in the mining and resources sector, with experience at Mine Australia, Mine Technology and Power Technology.

article_items.title: Watch AI Rally Fuels Tech Optimism | Open Interest 6/9/2026 - Bloomberg
article_items.description: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." The AI rebound rolls on as investors stay bullish on tech. OpenAI confidentially files for an IPO, SpaceX readies its blockbuster debut, and Apple faces doubts over its AI push. Plus, some optimism in the housing market as existing home sales accelerate, Emily Graffeo joins Open Interest on Apollo and Blackstone’s $35 billion AI financing deal, and Firefly Aerospace CEO Jason Kim on the space economy.
